vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of severe degradation. The expected specimens (P1-P4) are not visible as healthy, established plants. The environment is dominated by soil and debris.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No visible String of Nickels foliage.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No visible Mexican Mint foliage.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Systemic Loss: Pothos is absent; only the white rabbit anchor remains.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No visible Silver Guest foliage.

plant_audit.P1: Empty pot with soil and a sensor probe.
plant_audit.P3: Contains a white rabbit figurine; no plant matter present.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, damp, and undisturbed across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: White, crystalline fragments (likely perlite or mineral buildup) are present on the surface of the foreground pot.
biome_observations.surface_anomalies: A white cylindrical object (likely a supplement or chemical container) is present near the yellow pot.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: Over the 5-day sequence, there has been zero vegetative growth or recovery. The pots remain in a state of total dormancy or death. The only change is the slight shift in lighting and the positioning of the white cylindrical object.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-viable. There is no evidence of photosynthesis or active plant life. The specimens are either dead or have been removed, leaving only the substrate and anchors.
anomalies: Presence of a white cylindrical object (New Intervention).
Total absence of expected botanical specimens.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit confirms a complete systemic failure of the botanical collection. The pots contain only soil and inert anchors. No plant life is detected. The environment appears to be a sterile or failed experimental setup.
